title: Mature Aerated Australian Long-Fermentation Dough Flatbread
description: A nostalgic Australian flatbread featuring a floral, aerated texture and long-fermentation dough, perfect for ayurvedic sattvic meals.
instructions: Combine warm water, sugar, and yeast in a large mixing bowl. Let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy.
Add whole wheat flour, salt, and ground cloves to the bowl. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it ferment in a warm place for 24 hours.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces. Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Brush the dough with melted coconut oil.
Cook the flatbread for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's golden brown and puffed.
Brush the flatbread with rose petal jam and sprinkle with black sesame seeds, if desired.
